app.title: Meeco SVX Guide
app.intro: A friendly, step-by-step guide to the Meeco Portal, the SVX Wallet and SVX Verify. Follow the path in order, or jump in anywhere you like.

ui.glossaryIntro: Plain-language definitions for every term used in this guide. Hover any underlined word elsewhere to see its meaning.
ui.draftNotice: Draft content — placeholder copy to be replaced with your final text.

portal.title: Portal
portal.blurb: Set up your space, company and apps. Do this first.

wallet-setup.title: Wallet setup
wallet-setup.blurb: Stand up the wallet before you use it.

tenant.title: Create a Tenant
tenant.blurb: Your own private space on the platform.
tenant.body: A tenant is your own private, walled-off area on the Meeco platform. Think of it like renting your own apartment in a big building: everything inside — your credentials, settings and data — is yours and kept separate from everyone else. This is the very first thing you create.
steps: Sign in to the Meeco Portal

organisation.title: Add an Organisation
organisation.blurb: The company or team that owns the setup.
organisation.body: An organisation is the company or team identity that lives inside your tenant. It's the brand that will issue and verify credentials — the 'who' behind everything you do here.
steps: Fill in its name and details

applications.title: Create an Application / Deployment
applications.blurb: Switch on the services you'll use.
applications.body: Applications (also called deployments) are the actual running services connected to your organisation — like your Wallet. Creating one switches your setup on and gives you the details other tools use to connect.

api-keys.title: Create API Keys
api-keys.blurb: Secret keys so software can connect safely.
api-keys.body: API keys are secret passwords that let your software talk to the Wallet securely. Treat them like the keys to your house — keep them safe and never share them publicly.
steps: Copy and store it somewhere safe

admin-accounts.title: Add Admin Accounts
admin-accounts.blurb: Decide who can manage the Wallet.
admin-accounts.body: Admin accounts are the people allowed to log in and manage the Wallet. You decide who gets access to the control room and what they're allowed to do.

wallet-settings.title: Configure Wallet Settings
wallet-settings.blurb: How your wallet behaves and protects keys.
wallet-settings.body: Wallet settings control how your wallet behaves, including its encryption keys (the KMS). In SVX 4.0 the Wallet creates and looks after these keys for you, so there's much less to configure by hand.

schemas.title: Define Schemas
schemas.body: A schema is the blueprint for a credential. It lists the fields a credential must contain — for example 'full name' and 'date of birth'. Everything you issue starts from a schema.

credential-templates.title: Build Credential Templates
credential-templates.blurb: Turn a schema into something you can issue.
credential-templates.body: A credential template turns a schema into a ready-to-issue credential. It adds how the credential looks and the rules for handing it out — a reusable recipe you'll use again and again.

issued-credentials.title: Issue & View Credentials
issued-credentials.blurb: The real credentials you've handed out.
issued-credentials.body: Issued credentials are the real credentials you've created and given to people (holders). This is where you can see, track and manage every credential you've issued.

verification-templates.title: Create Verification Templates
verification-templates.blurb: Describe what you want to check.
verification-templates.body: A verification template describes what you want to check: which credential a person should hold and exactly which fields they need to show you. It's the question you ask before someone proves something.

verification-submissions.title: Review Verification Submissions
verification-submissions.body: Verification submissions are the answers people send back when you ask them to prove something. Here you review what they shared and confirm whether it passed your checks.

svx-configuration.title: Configure SVX Verify
svx-configuration.body: SVX Verify is Meeco's identity-checking service. Configuration is where you connect it and tune how it works for your own verification flows.

portal.label: Portal
portal.def: The Meeco Portal — the website where you set up and manage your tenant, organisation and applications.

tenant.label: Tenant
tenant.def: Your own private, isolated space on the Meeco platform where your data and settings live.

organisation.label: Organisation
organisation.def: The company or team identity that issues and verifies credentials inside your tenant.

application.label: Application
application.def: A running service (like the Wallet) connected to your organisation. Sometimes called a deployment.

deployment.label: Deployment
deployment.def: A live instance of an application that you've switched on and connected.

wallet.label: Wallet
wallet.def: The SVX Wallet — one unified service that issues, holds and verifies credentials.

api-key.label: API key
api-key.def: A secret key that lets your software connect to the Wallet securely.

admin-account.label: Admin account
admin-account.def: A login for a person who is allowed to manage the Wallet.

kms.label: KMS
kms.def: Key Management Service — the secure vault that creates and looks after your encryption keys.

verifiable-credential.label: Verifiable Credential
verifiable-credential.def: A tamper-proof digital claim about someone or something that others can check is genuine.

schema.label: Schema
schema.def: The blueprint listing the fields a credential must contain.

credential-template.label: Credential template
credential-template.def: A reusable recipe that turns a schema into an issuable credential, with display and rules.

issuer.label: Issuer
issuer.def: The party that creates and gives out credentials.

issued-credential.label: Issued credential
issued-credential.def: A credential that has actually been created and handed to a holder.

holder.label: Holder
holder.def: The person or thing that receives and keeps a credential in their wallet.

verification-template.label: Verification template
verification-template.def: A definition of what information a verifier asks a holder to show.

verifier.label: Verifier
verifier.def: The party that requests and checks credentials.

presentation-request.label: Presentation request
presentation-request.def: The request a verifier sends asking a holder to share specific credential information.

presentation-response.label: Presentation response
presentation-response.def: The holder's reply containing the requested credential information.

svx-verify.label: SVX Verify
svx-verify.def: Meeco's identity verification service, available through the platform.
